title: nephrolepis auriculata
intro: Nephrolepis auriculata, also known as Golden Boston fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lomariopsidaceae and is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Nephrolepis auriculata include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
